sourceMovie.title: The Voyeurs
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Voyeurs works as a thriller movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Se7en
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of The Voyeurs who respond to its thriller will find Se7en (1995) immediately familiar. Both films share a crime sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Whiplash
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason The Voyeurs fans keep landing on Whiplash (2014) — the thriller is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Mission: Impossible
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Mission: Impossible draws from the same thriller well as The Voyeurs, channeling adventure in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1996 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Mission: Impossible III
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Mission: Impossible III (2006) captures the thriller appeal of The Voyeurs without simply imitating it. The adventure streak running through both films is what puts Mission: Impossible III at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Eyes Wide Shut
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The thriller appeal of The Voyeurs — that tense edge, the drama — translates cleanly into Eyes Wide Shut (1999). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Enemy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Enemy (2014) clearly understood what makes thriller work — the same quality that keeps The Voyeurs rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Rosemary's Baby
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Rosemary's Baby (1968) is the kind of film The Voyeurs fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The thriller overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Basic Instinct
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Basic Instinct shares The Voyeurs's gift for thriller — the 1992 film delivers mystery without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: The Neon Demon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes The Voyeurs compelling — the thriller, the horror — shows up in The Neon Demon (2016) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Blue Velvet
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The thriller energy of The Voyeurs is alive in Blue Velvet (1986), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Voyeurs?
faq.answer: Try Se7en, Whiplash, Mission: Impossible—each shares DNA with The Voyeurs while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Voyeurs?
faq.answer: The Voyeurs (2021) is best described as Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Voyeurs worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
